title: The Heart Healed And Changed By Mercy.
content:
Sin enslaved me many years,
    And led me bound and blind;
    Till at length a thousand fears
    Came swarming o�er my mind.


    �Where,� I said, in deep distress,
    �Will these sinful pleasures end?
    How shall I secure my peace,
    And make the Lord my friend?�


    Friends and ministers said much
    The gospel to enforce;
    But my blindness still was such,
    I chose a legal course:
    Much I fasted, watch�d, and strove,
    Scarce would show my face abroad,
    Fear�d almost to speak or move,
    A stranger still to God.


    Thus afraid to trust his grace,
    Long time did I rebel;
    Till, despairing of my case,
    Down at his feet I fell:
    Then my stubborn heart he broke,
    And subdued me to his sway;
    By a simple word he spoke,
    �Thy sins are done away.�
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Cowper delivers a powerful performance in "The Heart Healed And Changed By Mercy."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
